tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P1] Create dialog search schema and mergeDialogSearch helper
tasks.description:
Create the shared Zod schema and merge helper that powers all dialog deep links.

**File to create:** `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/dialog-search-schema.ts`

**Full file contents:**

```ts
import { z } from 'zod';
import type { ZodObject, ZodRawShape } from 'zod';

/**
 * URL search params for deep-linking modal dialogs.
 *
 * Merged into every route's `validateSearch` schema via `mergeDialogSearch`
 * so dialog deep links work from any page without route-specific wiring.
 *
 * Each dialog uses two patterns:
 *  - Boolean-ish: `?tasks=open` opens the dialog (any non-empty value works,
 *    but `'open'` is the canonical form for parameterless dialogs)
 *  - Tab-targeted: `?settings=tools` opens the dialog to a specific tab
 *
 * Sub-section anchors use a sibling param (e.g. `?settings=tools&settingsSection=mcp`).
 */
export const dialogSearchSchema = z.object({
  // Settings
  settings: z.string().optional(),
  settingsSection: z.string().optional(),
  // Agent dialog
  agent: z.string().optional(),
  agentPath: z.string().optional(),
  // Other dialogs (parameterless — no tabs)
  tasks: z.string().optional(),
  relay: z.string().optional(),
  mesh: z.string().optional(),
});

export type DialogSearch = z.infer<typeof dialogSearchSchema>;

/**
 * Merge dialog search params into a route's existing search schema.
 *
 * @example
 * const sessionSearchSchema = mergeDialogSearch(
 *   z.object({ session: z.string().optional(), dir: z.string().optional() })
 * );
 */
export function mergeDialogSearch<T extends ZodRawShape>(routeSchema: ZodObject<T>) {
  return routeSchema.merge(dialogSearchSchema);
}
```

**Why each piece:**
- Each param uses `z.string().optional()` (not enum) to allow extension-registered tab IDs and forward compatibility. Resolved in spec §13 Q3.
- `mergeDialogSearch` is generic over `ZodRawShape` so it preserves the original route's typed schema.

**Pre-flight check (must run before writing):** grep `apps/client/src/router.tsx` for any existing route `validateSearch` that already declares `settings`, `agent`, `tasks`, `relay`, or `mesh` keys. Document the result. None should match per spec §13 Q5; if one does, surface it as a blocker.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] File created at exact path with TSDoc on every export
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (TSDoc rule satisfied)
- [ ] Pre-flight grep recorded in commit message; no conflicts found
tasks.activeForm: Creating dialog search schema and mergeDialogSearch helper

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P1] Export new schema and hooks from shared/model barrel
tasks.description:
Re-export the new schema, types, and hooks from the shared model barrel so the rest of the app can import them via `@/layers/shared/model`.

**File to modify:** `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/index.ts`

**Add the following exports:**

```ts
export {
  dialogSearchSchema,
  mergeDialogSearch,
  type DialogSearch,
} from './dialog-search-schema';

export {
  useSettingsDeepLink,
  useAgentDialogDeepLink,
  useOpenAgentDialog,
  useTasksDeepLink,
  useRelayDeepLink,
  useMeshDeepLink,
  type DialogDeepLink,
} from './use-dialog-deep-link';

export { useDeepLinkScroll } from './use-deep-link-scroll';
```

**FSD rule:** all consumers must import from this barrel, never from internal paths (per `.claude/rules/fsd-layers.md`).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All seven hooks/types exported from the barrel
- [ ] `import { useSettingsDeepLink } from '@/layers/shared/model'` resolves at consumer sites
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` green
- [ ] `pnpm lint` green

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P2] Declare urlParam on DIALOG_CONTRIBUTIONS entries
tasks.description:
Wire each dialog contribution to its URL param.

**File to modify:** `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/app-layout/model/dialog-contributions.ts`

**Updated array (replace existing definition verbatim):**

```ts
export const DIALOG_CONTRIBUTIONS: DialogContribution[] = [
  {
    id: 'settings',
    component: SettingsDialogWrapper,
    openStateKey: 'settingsOpen',
    urlParam: 'settings',
    priority: 1,
  },
  {
    id: 'directory-picker',
    component: DirectoryPickerWrapper,
    openStateKey: 'pickerOpen',
    priority: 2,
  },
  {
    id: 'tasks',
    component: TasksDialogWrapper,
    openStateKey: 'tasksOpen',
    urlParam: 'tasks',
    priority: 3,
  },
  {
    id: 'relay',
    component: RelayDialogWrapper,
    openStateKey: 'relayOpen',
    urlParam: 'relay',
    priority: 4,
  },
  {
    id: 'mesh',
    component: MeshDialogWrapper,
    openStateKey: 'meshOpen',
    urlParam: 'mesh',
    priority: 5,
  },
  {
    id: 'agent',
    component: AgentDialogWrapper,
    openStateKey: 'agentDialogOpen',
    urlParam: 'agent',
    priority: 6,
  },
];
```

**Important:** `directory-picker` does NOT get a `urlParam` — it's not deep-linkable across the app (it's opened from inside other dialogs and has its own ephemeral state). Per spec §6.6.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All five user-facing dialogs declare `urlParam`
- [ ] `directory-picker` left without `urlParam`
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` green
- [ ] No new lint warnings

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P2] Migrate command palette callsites to URL deep-link hooks
tasks.description:
Replace store-based open calls in command palette files with the new URL deep-link hooks.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/command-palette/model/use-palette-actions.ts`
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/command-palette/model/use-global-palette.ts`

**Migration pattern (per spec §6.9):**
- Replace each `setSettingsOpen(true)` / `openSettingsToTab('tools')` with `useSettingsDeepLink().open('tools')`
- Replace each `setTasksOpen(true)` with `useTasksDeepLink().open()`
- Replace each `setRelayOpen(true)` with `useRelayDeepLink().open()`
- Replace each `setMeshOpen(true)` with `useMeshDeepLink().open()`
- For agent dialog opens, use `useOpenAgentDialog()(path, tab?)`

**Important constraint:** If a palette action is defined inside a non-hook utility, refactor the action provider to call the hook at the top of a React component/hook and pass the resulting callbacks down. Hooks cannot be called inside arbitrary functions. Adjust as needed to keep rules-of-hooks satisfied.

**Steps:**
1. Read both files and inventory every `setXxxOpen(true)` / `openXxxToTab(...)` call
2. Map each to its hook equivalent
3. Hoist hook calls to the top of the consuming React hook/component
4. Wire callbacks through to the palette command list
5. Verify all existing palette commands still work

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] No `setSettingsOpen|setTasksOpen|setRelayOpen|setMeshOpen|openSettingsToTab|openAgentDialogToTab` calls remain in either file
- [ ] All palette commands open the corresponding dialog with the URL updated
- [ ] Manual smoke: open palette via Cmd+K, run "Settings", verify URL becomes `?settings=open`
- [ ] Existing palette tests pass (`command-palette-integration.test.tsx`)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint` + `pnpm test -- --run` green
tasks.activeForm: Migrating command palette callsites to URL deep-link hooks

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P2] Migrate sidebar and dashboard callsites to URL deep-link hooks
tasks.description:
Replace store-based open calls in dashboard health cards, session sidebar contributions, and connections/tasks views with the new hooks.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/dashboard-status/ui/SystemStatusRow.tsx` — health card click handlers
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/ConnectionsView.tsx`
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/TasksView.tsx` — replace open Tasks with `useTasksDeepLink().open()`
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/model/sidebar-contributions.ts` — sidebar buttons
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/model/use-task-notifications.ts` — open Tasks from notification

**Migration pattern (per spec §6.9):**
- Each `setXxxOpen(true)` / `openXxxToTab(...)` becomes the corresponding `useXxxDeepLink().open(...)` call
- For non-hook files (`sidebar-contributions.ts`), the contribution registry must accept callbacks that the host component (which can use hooks) supplies. Refactor the contribution shape if needed so the consuming component constructs the callback list with hooks at the top.
- `use-task-notifications.ts` is already a hook, so call `useTasksDeepLink()` at its top.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All five files migrated
- [ ] No `setXxxOpen|openXxxToTab` calls remain in these files
- [ ] Manual smoke: click each health card on dashboard, verify dialog opens with URL
- [ ] Manual smoke: click sidebar Tasks button, verify URL becomes `?tasks=open`
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger a task notification toast and click it, verify Tasks dialog opens
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint` + `pnpm test -- --run` green
tasks.activeForm: Migrating sidebar and dashboard callsites to URL deep-link hooks

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P3] Update SettingsDialog and AgentDialog tests for URL hook
tasks.description:
Refresh the existing tests so they mock `useSearch`/`useNavigate` (or wrap with a memory router) and continue to pass after the dialog tab sync was rewritten.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/__tests__/SettingsDialog.test.tsx`
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/__tests__/AgentDialog.test.tsx`

**Per spec §8.5:**
| Test | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `SettingsDialog.test.tsx` | Update mocks for `useSearch`/`useNavigate` since dialog now reads URL |
| `AgentDialog.test.tsx` | Same |

**Approach:** prefer wrapping with a TanStack Router test harness over `vi.mock('@tanstack/react-router')`. The harness keeps tests close to real behavior and lets tests drive the URL the same way as `DialogHost.test.tsx`.

**Steps:**
1. Add a router wrapper or extend the existing test harness to drive URL search
2. Replace any direct `vi.mock("@/layers/shared/model/app-store/...")` calls that mocked `settingsInitialTab` / `agentDialogInitialTab` with router navigation
3. Add new assertions: opening with URL `?settings=tools` selects Tools tab; switching tabs while URL-open updates the URL
4. Verify all original assertions still pass

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Both test files compile and pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/__tests__/SettingsDialog.test.tsx` green
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/__tests__/AgentDialog.test.tsx` green
tasks.activeForm: Updating SettingsDialog and AgentDialog tests for URL hook

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P4] Document URL deep linking in contributing/architecture.md and state-management.md
tasks.description:
Document the new URL deep-linking pattern in the contributor guides so future work uses the hooks instead of the deprecated store actions.

**Files to modify:**
- `contributing/architecture.md` — add a new section "Dialog deep linking via URL search params" describing the dual-signal pattern, the per-dialog hook API, and an example URL
- `contributing/state-management.md` — replace the existing guidance to use `openSettingsToTab` etc. with a pointer to `useSettingsDeepLink`, `useTasksDeepLink`, `useRelayDeepLink`, `useMeshDeepLink`, `useAgentDialogDeepLink`, and `useOpenAgentDialog`

**Section to add to architecture.md (per spec §11):**
```markdown
### Dialog deep linking via URL search params

DorkOS dialogs (Settings, Agent, Tasks, Relay, Mesh) are URL-addressable via search params on every route. The `dialogSearchSchema` is merged into each route's `validateSearch` schema; consumers use the per-dialog hooks in `@/layers/shared/model`:

- `useSettingsDeepLink()` — Settings dialog with tab + sub-section deep links
- `useAgentDialogDeepLink()` + `useOpenAgentDialog()` — Agent dialog with `agentPath`
- `useTasksDeepLink()` / `useRelayDeepLink()` / `useMeshDeepLink()` — parameterless dialogs

`RegistryDialog` reads BOTH the URL signal and the existing store flag (`storeOpen || urlSignal.isOpen`) so legacy store-based opens continue to work. Closing the dialog clears both signals. Use the URL hooks for any new cross-page open.

Example URLs:
- `/?settings=tools` — Settings on Dashboard, Tools tab
- `/agents?settings=tools&settingsSection=external-mcp` — Settings on Agents page, Tools tab, scrolled to External MCP
- `/?agent=identity&agentPath=/abs/path/to/repo` — Agent dialog → Identity for that project
```

**Update for state-management.md:**
- Find the section describing `openSettingsToTab` / store-based dialog opens
- Replace with: "Use the deep-link hooks from `@/layers/shared/model` (`useSettingsDeepLink`, `useTasksDeepLink`, `useRelayDeepLink`, `useMeshDeepLink`, `useAgentDialogDeepLink`, `useOpenAgentDialog`) for cross-page dialog opens. Store-based opens via `setSettingsOpen` etc. remain valid as a fallback for in-dialog flows that don't need URL state."

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] New section added to `contributing/architecture.md`
- [ ] `contributing/state-management.md` updated to point at new hooks
- [ ] `docs:reconcile` (manual review) confirms no stale references to `openSettingsToTab`/`openAgentDialogToTab`
tasks.activeForm: Documenting URL deep linking in contributor guides

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P4] Add changelog entry for URL-addressable dialogs
tasks.description:
Add a changelog entry announcing the new URL deep-linking surface.

**Per spec §11 and §7:**

**Changelog text:**
```
Settings, Tasks, Relay, Mesh, and Agent dialogs are now URL-addressable via search params. Share links like `?settings=tools` to deep-link teammates to a specific dialog and tab. Browser back closes dialogs; reload preserves dialog state.

Note: Sharing a deep link with `?agentPath=...` includes your local project path.
```

**Steps:**
1. Locate the changelog file (typically `CHANGELOG.md` or via `/changelog:backfill` skill)
2. Add an `Added` / `Changed` bullet under the Unreleased section using the wording above
3. Include the privacy note about `agentPath` per spec §10

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Changelog entry added
- [ ] Wording mentions all five dialogs (Settings, Tasks, Relay, Mesh, Agent)
- [ ] Privacy note about `agentPath` included
- [ ] Format matches existing changelog entries
tasks.activeForm: Adding changelog entry for URL-addressable dialogs

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-03-url-deeplinks] [P4] Run final verification gate (typecheck/test/lint/browsertest + manual smokes)
tasks.description:
Run the full verification gate from spec §12 Phase 8 before considering the spec complete.

**Automated checks:**
1. `pnpm typecheck` — must be green
2. `pnpm test -- --run` — must be green
3. `pnpm lint` — must be green
4. `pnpm browsertest` — must be green

**Manual smoke tests (per spec §12 Phase 8):**
5. Copy `?settings=tools` URL into a fresh tab — verify Settings dialog opens to Tools tab
6. Navigate to `/agents`, click Settings in palette — verify URL shows `?settings=open`
7. Open Settings via deep link, hit browser back — verify dialog closes
8. Navigate to `?settings=tools&settingsSection=external-mcp` — verify External MCP is scrolled into view (and expanded if applicable)
9. Navigate to `?agent=identity&agentPath=/abs/path/to/repo` — verify Agent dialog opens to Identity tab for that project
10. Open Settings via store (e.g., `useAppStore.getState().setSettingsOpen(true)`) — verify dialog opens without polluting URL (dual-signal fallback works)

**Stop condition:** if any check fails, file a follow-up task with the specific failure, do NOT mark this task complete until everything is green.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All four automated checks green
- [ ] All six manual smokes verified by screenshot or note
- [ ] No regressions in existing dialog flows
- [ ] Verification record (output snippets) saved or linked in the spec PR description
tasks.activeForm: Running final verification gate
